description_long: This stone block is irregularly shaped with a roughly cuboidal form, featuring uneven and jagged surfaces. Its coloration is a mix of earthy browns, tans, and off-whites, with visible patches of lighter and darker mineral deposits. The surface texture is coarse and pitted, with some areas appearing more weathered and eroded than others, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. There are no visible engravings or markings, but some faces show natural fractures and chips. The block appears solid and heavy, likely carved or broken from a larger stone mass. Its size and form make it suitable as a barrier or obstacle in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: An irregular, coarse stone block with earthy brown and tan coloration, featuring rough, weathered surfaces and natural chips.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, relatively flat face with a prominent lighter patch and angular edges.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a more rounded, pitted surface with subtle color variations and a visible depression.
description_view_2: This side reveals a lighter, more uniform face with less pronounced chipping and a slightly concave area.
description_view_3: This view displays a blocky, angular face with a mix of light and dark patches and visible surface roughness.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or placed directly on compacted soil or gravel
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with chipped corners, pitting, and some color variation from exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or loosely grouped with other stones
